description_long: This is a small, rustic log cabin constructed from dark, rounded wooden logs stacked horizontally, with the log ends protruding at the corners. The roof is a steeply pitched gable style, covered in dark, textured shingles that extend slightly beyond the walls, providing a modest overhang. The front facade features a single, open rectangular doorway without a visible door, and no windows are present on any side. The side and rear walls are solid, emphasizing the robust, insulated nature of the structure. The overall appearance is compact and sturdy, suitable for use as a small shelter or outbuilding in a park or residential setting. The cabin's design is simple and functional, with a focus on durability and traditional log construction.
description: A compact, dark wooden log cabin with a steep gable shingle roof and a single open doorway, featuring no windows and solid log walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open rectangular doorway and the log construction details on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid log wall and the overhanging gable roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a completely solid log wall beneath the gable roof, with no openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side, with a solid log wall and the gable roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in dark asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are present on any visible side; all walls are solid logs with no openings except the front doorway.
attribute_door_placement: A single open doorway is centered on the front facade; no other doors are visible.
